2606.27409.title: Delayed Verification Destabilizes Multi-Agent LLM Belief: Instability Thresholds and Optimal Corrector Placement
2606.27409.paper_summary: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems often rely on verifier and critic agents to suppress hallucinations, but verification is delayed. During this delay, false claims can propagate through the agent network. We model this process as delayed consensus on a graph with grounded corrector nodes. Spectral decomposition by the grounded Laplacian yields a closed-form stability threshold for the verification dose: correction that is too strong or too delayed can turn consensus into oscillation. The most unstable regime occurs when the communication and verification delays coincide; for delay two, the threshold is the inverse golden ratio. The same framework gives a supermodular placement objective and a greedy (1-1/e)-approximation rule for assigning a limited corrector budget to influential nodes. Experiments across five open models confirm the predicted dose-delay oscillations. By contrast, grounded factual answering makes truth an absorbing boundary and eliminates the effect, suggesting that the instability is specific to signed-belief tasks while grounded verification remains stabilizing
2606.27409.paper_summary_zh: 多代理大型語言模型（LLM）系統通常依賴驗證者和評價者代理來抑制幻覺，但驗證存在延遲。<lb/>在這段延遲期間，虛假聲明可以在代理網絡中傳播。我們將這個過程建模為在具有基礎校正節點的圖上的延遲共識。<lb/>通過基礎拉普拉斯的譜分解，可以得出驗證劑量的封閉形式穩定性閾值：過強或過遲的校正會使共識變為振盪。<lb/>當通信和驗證延遲重合時，最不穩定的狀態發生；對於延遲為二的情況，閾值為逆黃金比例。<lb/>相同的框架給出了超模塊放置目標和一個貪婪的（1-1/e）近似規則，用於將有限的校正預算分配給有影響力的節點。<lb/>在五個開放模型上的實驗確認了預測的劑量-延遲振盪。<lb/>相比之下，基礎事實回答使真相成為吸收邊界並消除了這一效應，這表明不穩定性特定於簽名信念任務，而基礎驗證仍保持穩定。
2606.27409.author: Igor Itkin et.al.
